title: Trump and Rubio Insist Iran War Is Over, Even as Missiles Fly During Cease-Fire
summary: The White House is turning to rhetorical leaps as President Trump tries to put the biggest political crisis of his presidency behind him.

title: President Trump’s Sudden U-Turn, and a $1 Billion Ballroom Proposal
summary: Plus, a visit to the “Gates to Hell.”

title: In Indiana Primary Elections, Most Trump-Backed Challengers Beat Incumbents
summary: President Trump endorsed primary opponents against seven Republican lawmakers who opposed his redistricting push. At least five of the Trump-backed candidates have won their races.

title: Takeaways From Indiana Primary Elections 2026: Trump Gets Payback
summary: The president helped unseat most of the state lawmakers he targeted after they rebuffed his call to draw new House maps to help Republicans.

title: Tofersen, a New Treatment for A.L.S., Reverses Symptoms for Some
summary: The drug is for a small subset of patients. But evidence that breathing and strength can get better for some of them is remarkable for a paralyzing, usually fatal disease.

title: A Look Inside the Case That Enshrined Political Power for Billionaires
summary: After Watergate, Congress tried to curtail the role of money in politics. But a pivotal Supreme Court case nipped it in the bud. Years later, new details are emerging on how wealthy Americans were conferred with a “right to spend” on elections.

title: What Happened When Pope Leo XIV Had to Call Customer Service
summary: Spoiler alert: There was no miracle.

title: World Cup Dynamic Ticket Pricing Drives Argentine Soccer Fans To Extremes
summary: Dynamic ticket pricing has made this year’s tournament the most expensive in history, pushing Argentine fans to extremes.

title: Guy Goma’s Accidental BBC Interview Lives On After 20 Years
summary: Guy Goma thought he was interviewing for an I.T. job when he found himself on air on the BBC. What came next was familiar to anyone who has been unprepared at work.

title: 3 People Are Evacuated From Cruise Ship With Hantavirus Outbreak
summary: Three people with possible symptoms of the disease were evacuated on Wednesday morning from the Dutch-flagged MV Hondius.

title: In Romania, Living With Weapons of War Spilling Into NATO Territory
summary: Four years of battle in Ukraine is taking a toll on neighboring Romanians. Even if the war has not crossed the border, drones have done so.

title: Deadly Russian Strikes Rip Into Ukrainian Cities
summary: At least 12 people were killed in Zaporizhzhia, one of the highest tolls from a single attack so far this year. The cities of Dnipro and Kramatorsk were also attacked.

title: American Factories Lag in Adopting A.I. This Drugmaker Is an Exception.
summary: A Bristol Myers Squibb plant that makes cancer drugs was the only manufacturer in the U.S. recognized for innovation by the World Economic Forum this year.

title: Japan’s Takaichi Pitches Strength and Stability in Visits to Australia and Vietnam
summary: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is attempting to raise Japan’s profile at a time when China’s clout is rising and doubts about the United States are spreading.

title: To Rename Its Airport After Trump, Palm Beach Had to License His Name
summary: The Trumps won’t profit from airport-branded merchandise sold on site. But the agreement doesn’t prevent them from profiting off such items sold elsewhere.

title: Howard Lutnick to Face Questions From Congress About Epstein Ties
summary: The commerce secretary is set to be interviewed Wednesday in a closed-door session with the House Oversight Committee

title: U.S. Military Strikes Boat in Eastern Pacific, Killing 3
summary: The attack was the second in two days, and the latest in an ongoing campaign against people the United States says are engaged in drug smuggling at sea.

title: Renewables Are Gaining on Fossil Fuels, IRENA Report Finds
summary: Sources like wind and solar can now deliver continuous power, according to a new report. And, they’re often a bargain compared with fossil fuels.

title: John Roberts Believes in an America That Doesn’t Exist
summary: Guess who benefits from a “colorblind Constitution.”

title: If Kids Need GLP-1s, We’ve Already Let Them Down
summary: The debate over treatment in kids is missing a bigger problem.

title: The Cruise From Hell
summary: We look into the hantavirus crisis onboard the Hondius.

title: Late Night Cringes at Trump’s Chatter in Front of Children
summary: “How are you talking about nuclear war in front of children?” Desi Lydic asked after the president discussed the Iran conflict in a room full of students on Tuesday.

title: Illinois State Police to Investigate Fatal ICE Shooting
summary: Officials said they were examining the shooting of Silverio Villegas-Gonzalez last summer during a Chicago-area crackdown on illegal immigration.

title: Trial Begins for Man Accused of Running Secret Police Outpost for China
summary: Lu Jianwang, a U.S. citizen, is accused of working on behalf of the Chinese government to target dissidents. A co-defendant has pleaded guilty.

title: How Did a State Auditor Become the ‘People’s Rockstar’?
summary: Massachusetts voters approved a ballot measure in 2024 authorizing Diana DiZoglio, the state auditor, to closely scrutinize the State Legislature. Lawmakers are resisting.

title: Pollution Worsened in South Bronx After Congestion Tolls, Study Finds
summary: Columbia University researchers said that sensors detected a 2 percent increase in toxic particles in the air. M.T.A. officials disputed the report.
